svg.text: 8-Phase Recovery: Failure Detection → Promotion → Service Update → Debezium Reconnect → Offset Resume

g.text: Creates new slot on new primary
App connects via ecom-db (ExternalName) → ecom-db-rw → Primary pod
Debezium reads WAL via logical replication slot | Offsets stored in Kafka
Streaming replication: Primary → Standby (synchronous WAL shipping)
Primary pod crashes, gets OOMKilled, or node goes down
App JDBC connections break immediately (TCP RST) | Debezium loses TCP connection
Standby continues running with latest replicated WAL data
Instance manager reports unhealthy | Reconcile loop detects pod not ready
CNPG evaluates cluster topology: 1 standby available for promotion
Decision: promote ecom-db-2 (most up-to-date standby based on WAL position)
pg_ctl promote on ecom-db-2 | Standby exits recovery mode
New primary begins accepting write transactions | Timeline ID increments
CNPG updates instance labels: cnpg.io/instanceRole=primary on ecom-db-2
CNPG patches ecom-db-rw Service: Endpoints now point to ecom-db-2 pod IP
ExternalName alias (ecom-db) resolves to ecom-db-rw → new primary transparently
Zero configuration change needed in any application or Debezium config
HikariCP / connection pool detects broken connections → creates new ones
DNS resolves ecom-db → ecom-db-rw → new primary pod IP
Application resumes normal operation — users experience brief (~5-15s) interruption
Debezium reconnects to same hostname → reaches new primary
Creates new logical replication slot on new primary (old slot was on old primary)
Reads last committed offset from Kafka topic → resumes from that LSN position
CNPG recreates old primary pod as new standby | Streaming replication resumes
Cluster returns to 2 healthy instances (1 primary + 1 standby)
Total downtime: ~5-15 seconds | Zero data loss (synchronous replication)

svg.text: CloudNativePG v1.25.1 · Automatic Failover · KafkaOffsetBackingStore · ExternalName Service Aliases · Zero App Code Changes
